methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Nuvacore is a seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Gerard Williams III. The Chips & Semiconductors sector comprises 14 total peers, including 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms. Top private comparables include Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Two standard valuation methods do not yield reliable output for this target. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable density within the narrow peer set—only 2 adjacent-stage peers exist where a minimum of 3 is required for statistical validity. Public-comp anchoring cannot be performed because Nuvacore's revenue is not on file, and without this metric, applying the Semiconductors sector P/S median of 20.3x across the 3 public tickers produces only a theoretical bound rather than a defensible valuation.

Given these data constraints, a quantitative triangulation range cannot be responsibly constructed. The analysis instead identifies available benchmarks: the public sector trades at a 20.3x P/S median, and 8 private peers exist at varying stages. A forward valuation would require either disclosure of Nuvacore's projected revenue, detailed financials from its peer set, or stage-specific benchmarking data. The primary uncertainty is the absence of the target's own revenue metrics, which precludes standard venture-backed semiconductor valuation approaches.
